source: Bergeaud & Verluise (2023), PLOS ONE — the paper that built this gold set. Self-driving is defined at the FUNCTIONAL APPLICATION level (a set of tasks), not by keywords.
definition: Self-driving / autonomous vehicles: technology that enables a vehicle to make its OWN driving decisions and operate the dynamic driving task with reduced or NO human control (automate driving). The defining functional task is 'enabling cars to make autonomous decisions'.

primary_decision_axis.name: automate_vs_assist
primary_decision_axis.rule: The SAME underlying technology (camera, lidar, lane detection, control, V2X, CV) can serve EITHER automated driving OR human-driver assistance. ACCEPT (SEED) only if the vehicle itself drives / decides. REJECT (NOT_SEED) if a HUMAN driver stays in control and the invention merely assists, warns, or improves comfort — even if identical sensors/algorithms are used. This single distinction is why the gold set is hard (human inter-annotator and model F1 ~79%, vs ~90% for other domains).

in_scope_tasks.desc: the vehicle makes autonomous driving DECISIONS / controls the dynamic driving task itself (SAE L3-L5, self-driving, driverless)
environment perception (object/lane/sign detection, sensor fusion, lidar/radar/camera) explicitly FOR automated driving / an autonomous vehicle
motion/trajectory/path planning, behavior & decision planning for a self-driving vehicle
localization / HD-map / SLAM used by an autonomous vehicle to drive itself
V2X / connected-vehicle communication that enables or coordinates automated driving
teleoperation / remote driving of an autonomous vehicle (human not physically driving)

out_of_scope_confusables: ADAS / driver-assistance where the HUMAN keeps control: lane-departure WARNING, blind-spot ALERT, forward-collision WARNING, parking ASSIST, adaptive cruise as an assist feature
driver monitoring / drowsiness / attention systems (about the human, not autonomous driving)
general automotive engineering with no autonomous decision-making (powertrain, brakes, steering hardware, suspension, body, HVAC)
generic computer vision / object detection not applied to driving a vehicle
generic robotics / unmanned AERIAL / underwater / industrial robots / mowers (not on-road self-driving cars)
infotainment, human-driver navigation maps, insurance / telematics / data-logging that merely mentions autonomous vehicles
hard_negative_patterns: uses driving-perception or control tech but the HUMAN driver remains in control (assist/warn only) -> reject
obstacle / collision WARNING for a human-driven vehicle -> reject
same CV/lidar/control method described generically, vehicle only as an example, no autonomous driving -> reject
non-road autonomous robot (drone, UAV, AUV, mower, warehouse robot) -> reject
vehicle component (brake, motor, steering) with no autonomous-driving decision -> reject

score_anchors.0.9_1.0: the vehicle clearly AUTOMATES driving / makes autonomous decisions, with a concrete mechanism
score_anchors.0.6_0.75: driving-automation plausibly implied but the automate-vs-assist boundary is unclear, or mixed assist/autonomy signals -> boundary
score_anchors.0.0_0.25: human driver stays in control (assist/warn only), OR no autonomous-driving signal, OR only a confusable/out-of-domain signal
